name: Bilbao River Walk: Guggenheim to Old Town
name_original: Puente de San Antón y Catedral de Santiago desde Askatasuna — ida y vuelta por el Museo de Guggenheim
description: This easy 4.0-mile (6.4 km) urban hike in Bilbao connects historic Puente de San Antón, Santiago Cathedral, and the Guggenheim Museum.
region_intro: Hiking around Bilbao offers access to a diverse landscape of green mountains, dramatic coastlines, and expansive natural parks. The region features a mix of rugged limestone formations, dense forests, and coastal cliffs. This varied terrain provides numerous hiking opportunities, from urban river walks to challenging mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Bilbao The most popular hiking route is Bilbao City Highlights Trail, a 4.1 miles (6.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the city and its river. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ganekogorta from San Justo — circular by Monte Pagasarri, a difficult 12.1 miles (19.5 km) path. This trail involves significant elevation gain, leading to panoramic mountain views. Local hikers also love the Gaztelu/Kukurrustu from Basarrate — circular via Malmasin, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through hills and forests, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Hiking around Bilbao is defined by mountainous terrain, coastal routes, and natural parks like Urkiola and Gorbea. The extensive network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from gentle strolls to demanding ascents. The routes in Bilbao are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 900 reviews. More than 6000 hikers have used komoot to explore Bilbao's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Bilbao?
A: Bilbao and its surrounding region offer a wide variety of hiking experiences. You can find over 200 hiking routes on komoot, catering to all skill levels from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails around Bilbao?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails around Bilbao, with an average score of 4.4 stars from nearly 1000 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from lush green mountains to dramatic coastlines, and the well-maintained paths that offer stunning views of the city and natural parks.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking near Bilbao?
A: Hiking near Bilbao offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll find lush green mountains, such as those in Urkiola and Gorbea Natural Parks, with rugged limestone formations and deep valleys. The region also boasts dramatic coastlines featuring stunning cliffs, beautiful beaches, and estuaries, particularly around areas like Plentzia and Sopelana.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or casual walkers in Bilbao?
A: Yes, there are plenty of easy hiking options around Bilbao. For a pleasant urban experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056306986/bilbao-city-highlights-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bilbao City Highlights Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.7 km route offering views of the city and its river. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309739487/bilbao-river-walk-guggenheim-to-old-town" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bilbao River Walk: Guggenheim to Old Town&lt;/a&gt;, which is also easy and showcases the city's iconic landmarks.

Q: Where can I find more challenging hikes near Bilbao?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the mountainous terrain around Bilbao offers several demanding routes. A popular difficult trail is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309765831/ganekogorta-from-san-justo-circular-by-monte-pagasarri" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ganekogorta from San Justo — circular by Monte Pagasarri&lt;/a&gt;, a 19.5 km path with significant elevation gain leading to panoramic mountain views. Urkiola Natural Park also features more difficult treks, with notable peaks like Anboto (1,331 meters).

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Bilbao?
A: Yes, many trails around Bilbao are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309787228/gaztelu-kukurrustu-from-basarrate-circular-via-malmasin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaztelu/Kukurrustu from Basarrate — circular via Malmasin&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.1 km route through hills and forests. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309812534/mercator-de-la-ribera-from-bilbao-abando-circular-via-plaza-nueva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mercator de la Ribera from Bilbao-Abando — circular via Plaza Nueva&lt;/a&gt;, an easy city walk.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls near Bilbao?
A: Yes, the region around Bilbao is home to some impressive waterfalls. The Nervion Waterfall Route from Delika is an 8.6 km trail leading to the highest waterfall on the Iberian Peninsula, suitable for families. Additionally, Bolintxubidea offers a hidden waterfall trail near Bilbao, providing a lush, wild forest experience.

Q: What coastal hiking opportunities are there around Bilbao?
A: The Basque coast near Bilbao offers stunning coastal hikes. Trails around Plentzia reveal a striking collision of sea and mountains. The Bidezabal—Sopelana hike takes you along rugged cliffs overlooking the Atlantic, often starting and ending at beaches. The iconic San Juan de Gaztelugatxe also features scenic coastal routes.

Q: Are there any historical sites or natural monuments I can visit while hiking?
A: Absolutely. While hiking around Bilbao, you can discover several interesting landmarks. For instance, you might encounter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Butrón Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a fairytale-like fortress. The region also features natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ganekogorta Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive views, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4018034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hirumugarrieta Megalithic Site&lt;/a&gt; for a glimpse into ancient history.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Bilbao accessible by public transport?
A: Many trails around Bilbao are well-connected by public transport. Mount Artxanda, for example, is easily accessible from the city. For trails further afield, local bus services often connect to towns near natural parks like Urkiola and Gorbea, though some trailheads might require a short walk or taxi ride from the nearest stop.

Q: Are there dog-friendly hiking trails in the Bilbao region?
A: The Bilbao region generally welcomes dogs on trails, especially in natural parks and rural areas. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, particularly in protected areas like Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, and to check specific park regulations beforehand. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking around Bilbao?
A: The best time for hiking around Bilbao is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, with lush greenery in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. Summer can be warm, especially for mountain hikes, while winter can bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain, though coastal walks remain enjoyable.
tips: It is worth coming to see the view from the bridge.
This impressive Gothic-style cathedral with a cloister is an oasis of calm in this bustling city.

The Plaza Nueva is a beautiful neoclassical square that was built in 1821. This square is the lively heart of the Casco Viejo, the old district of Bilbao. Plaza Nueva is surrounded by buildings, where the lower floor consists of arches. Under these arches you will find numerous pintxos bars and restaurants. The square is therefore ideally suited to grab a terrace at the end of the afternoon and watch the Basque life while enjoying pintxos, the Basque form of tapas.

Also called the “Calatrava Bridge” after the architect who built it, it is an arched, white and slender structure that looks like a sailing ship ten meters high above the river mouth. Its glass surface has caused a lot of controversy, which is why today people step on a non-slip mat. It connects Uribarte with Campo Volantín and, despite the controversies that have always accompanied it, is one of the symbols of the city.
The Guggenheim museum not only cleaned the face of the dirty Bilbao of the 80's but also gave the city a modern and avant-garde air. A 'must' to visit in this city.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this urban hike?
faqs.answer: This easy urban hike, covering approximately 6.4 kilometers, usually takes about 1 hour and 42 minutes to complete at a leisurely pace. This allows ample time to enjoy the various landmarks along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain for this route is predominantly flat and paved, as it navigates through the city's urban landscape. It includes riverside walks and city streets, making it accessible for a wide range of walkers and suitable for a leisurely stroll.

faqs.question: What are the main landmarks I will see on this route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several iconic Bilbao landmarks, including the historic Puente de San Antón, the impressive Santiago Cathedral, the lively Plaza Nueva, the modern Zubizuri Bridge, and the world-renowned Guggenheim Museum. The route offers a compelling contrast between ancient and modern architecture.

faqs.question: Is this urban trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as an 'easy' walk, with minimal elevation changes and a well-maintained, paved path. This makes it very suitable for families with children, offering a comfortable way to explore Bilbao's cultural highlights.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to experience this walk?
faqs.answer: Bilbao generally has a mild climate, making this urban walk enjoyable for much of the year. Spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October) are often ideal, offering pleasant temperatures for walking and fewer crowds than peak summer months.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As an urban walking route through city streets and along the river, dogs are generally permitted, but they should always be kept on a leash. Be mindful of local regulations regarding dogs in specific parks or public buildings you might pass.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this urban walk?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to walk this urban route. It's a public path through the city. However, individual attractions like the Guggenheim Museum or Santiago Cathedral may have their own entrance fees if you choose to go inside.

faqs.question: Where can I find parking near the trailhead or along the route?
faqs.answer: Given its urban setting, parking can be found in various public and private parking garages throughout Bilbao's city center. For areas near the Guggenheim Museum or Puente de San Antón, paid parking facilities are usually available. Public transport is also a convenient option.

faqs.question: How can I reach the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: The starting point near Askatasuna, as well as other points along the route like Puente de San Antón or the Guggenheim Museum, are well-served by Bilbao's public transport network, including metro, tram, and bus lines. Check local transport maps for the most convenient stops.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. As an urban walk, you'll find numerous cafes, bars, and restaurants, especially around the Santiago Cathedral in the Casco Viejo (Old Town) and near the Guggenheim Museum. These offer plenty of opportunities for refreshments or a meal.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in Bilbao?
faqs.answer: Yes, this urban route intersects with several other paths. You'll find yourself on sections of trails such as Bilbao - Lutxana, the Northern Route (Basque Country), Posta kalea, and Campo Volantin pasealekua, among others, as you explore the city.

waypoints.name: View of San Antón Church and Bridge
